paper_title: Ice Flow and Monte Carlo Sampling in NEGIS

methods.description: A two-dimensional Dansgaard-Johnsen model is used to simulate the propagation and deformation of internal layers along approximated flow lines between the ice-sheet summit (GRIP) and East-GRIP. This model is chosen for its simplicity and suitability for the Monte Carlo method due to its few model parameters and analytical solution.

steps.description: Use a two-dimensional Dansgaard-Johnsen model to simulate ice flow.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate ice flow using Dansgaard-Johnsen model

steps.description: Assume ice incompressibility and a constant vertical strain rate down to the kink height, below which the strain rate decreases linearly.
steps.searchable_summary: apply ice incompressibility and strain rate assumptions

steps.description: Include basal sliding and melting in the model, assuming constant ice-sheet thickness over time.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate basal sliding and melting into model

methods.description: A Markov chain Monte Carlo method is used to determine the unknown parameters of a two-dimensional ice-flow model by minimizing the misfit between modeled and observed isochrones and strain rates. This probabilistic approach is well-suited for nonlinear problems with multiple maxima in the probability density.

steps.description: Use a random number generator to sample according to the target probability distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: implement random sampling for probability distribution

steps.description: Start from an initial model and use a random walker to explore the model space, proposing new models accepted with a certain probability.
steps.searchable_summary: explore model space with random walker

steps.description: Evaluate the quality of proposed models using a misfit function and accept new models based on acceptance probability.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate model quality and accept based on probability

steps.description: Discard the first 1×10^6 accepted models to avoid sampling during the burn-in phase and save every 1000th accepted model.
steps.searchable_summary: discard initial models and save every 1000th model
